title: corymbia stockeri subsp. peninsularis
intro: Corymbia stockeri subsp. peninsularis, also known as the Peninsular Stockyard Gum, is a subspecies of tree native to Western Australia. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Corymbia stockeri subsp. peninsularis include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
